entities.name: SESAMUM PROSTRATUM RETZ.
entities.description: An endemic plant species and crop wild relative of sesame found in coastal strand habitats, categorized as critically endangered.

entities.name: SESAME
entities.description: A cultivated crop for which Sesamum prostratum Retz. is a wild relative.

entities.name: COASTAL STRANDS
entities.description: A narrow strip of land adjacent to the seashore that serves as a niche habitat for endemic species.

entities.name: INDIAN SUB-CONTINENT
entities.description: A geographic region encompassing the eastern coasts of Southern India and Sri Lanka.

entities.name: SOUTHERN INDIA
entities.description: A region within the Indian sub-continent where Sesamum prostratum Retz. is found.

entities.name: SRI LANKA
entities.description: A country where Sesamum prostratum Retz. is endemic and declared critically endangered.

entities.name: SALINE TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability to tolerate high salt concentrations, potentially present in Sesamum prostratum Retz.

entities.name: CROP WILD RELATIVE
entities.description: A wild species related to a cultivated crop, valuable for crop improvement and diversification.

entities.name: IUCN GUIDELINES
entities.description: Standardized guidelines used for the risk assessment and categorization of endangered species.

entities.name: CRITICALLY ENDANGERED
entities.description: A conservation status indicating a species is facing an extremely high risk of extinction in the wild.

entities.name: TRANS SITU CONSERVATION
entities.description: A conservation strategy involving the management of species outside their natural habitat to prevent extinction.

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of enhancing the genetic traits of crops for better yield, quality, or resilience.

entities.name: TRAIT INTROGRESSION
entities.description: The process of introducing specific traits from a wild relative into a cultivated crop through crossing.

entities.name: CROP DIVERSIFICATION
entities.description: The process of increasing the variety of crops cultivated to enhance food security and resilience.

entities.name: DE NOVO DOMESTICATION
entities.description: The process of domesticating a wild species from scratch using modern genetic tools.

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A cultivated crop used to demonstrate the effectiveness of trait introgression and de novo domestication.

entities.name: TOMATO
entities.description: A cultivated crop used to demonstrate the effectiveness of trait introgression and de novo domestication.

entities.name: GENE EDITING TOOLS
entities.description: Molecular tools used to precisely modify DNA for trait introgression and de novo domestication.

entities.name: FRAGMENTED HABITAT
entities.description: A state where a species' habitat is broken into smaller, isolated patches, increasing extinction risk.
